_comment: Mock-exam question bank: the official USCIS civics questions as multiple choice. The 8 'answers vary' current-official questions are excluded (they can't be auto-graded). 'a' is the index of the correct option; 'ch' is the chapter number, resolved to a link by the build.

questions.q: What is the form of government of the United States?
What is the supreme law of the land?
Name one thing the U.S. Constitution does.
o: Sets up the government and protects rights

questions.q: What does 'We the People' mean?
o: Self-government — power comes from the people
The power of the states alone

questions.q: How are changes made to the U.S. Constitution?
o: By a simple act of Congress

questions.q: What does the Bill of Rights protect?
o: The basic rights of people in the United States

questions.q: How many amendments does the U.S. Constitution have?
Why is the Declaration of Independence important?
o: It said America is free from Britain and all people are created equal

questions.q: What founding document said the colonies were free from Britain?
Name one important idea from the Declaration and the Constitution.
The words 'Life, Liberty, and the pursuit of Happiness' are in what document?
What is the economic system of the United States?
What is the rule of law?
o: Everyone, including leaders, must obey the law
The President is above the law
Only citizens must follow the law
Laws can be ignored in emergencies

questions.q: Name one document that influenced the U.S. Constitution.
Why are there three branches of government?
o: So no one branch becomes too powerful (checks and balances)

questions.q: Name the three branches of government.
The President is in charge of which branch?
What part of the federal government writes laws?
What are the two parts of the U.S. Congress?
o: The Senate and the House of Representatives
The Senate and the Supreme Court

questions.q: Name one power of the U.S. Congress.
How many U.S. senators are there?
How long is a term for a U.S. senator?
How many voting members are in the House of Representatives?
How long is a term for a member of the House?
Why do U.S. representatives serve shorter terms than senators?
o: To more closely follow public opinion

questions.q: How many senators does each state have?
Why does each state have two senators?
Who does a U.S. senator represent?
Who elects U.S. senators?
Who does a member of the House represent?
Who elects members of the House?
Why do some states have more representatives than others?
The President is elected for how many years?
Why can the President serve only two terms?
If the President can no longer serve, who becomes President?
o: A new election is held at once

questions.q: Name one power of the President.
o: Signs or vetoes bills; Commander in Chief

questions.q: Who is Commander in Chief of the military?
Who signs bills to become laws?
Who vetoes bills?
Who appoints federal judges?
Name one part of the executive branch.
What does the President's Cabinet do?
Name a Cabinet-level position.
Why is the Electoral College important?
o: It decides who is elected President

questions.q: What is one part of the judicial branch?
What does the judicial branch do?
o: Reviews laws and decides if they follow the Constitution

questions.q: What is the highest court in the United States?
How many seats are on the Supreme Court?
How many justices are usually needed to decide a case?
How long do Supreme Court justices serve?
Why do Supreme Court justices serve for life?
Name one power that is only for the federal government.
Name one power that is only for the states.
What is the purpose of the 10th Amendment?
o: Powers not given to the federal government belong to the states or people

questions.q: Describe one of the amendments about who can vote.
Who can vote in federal elections, run for federal office, and serve on a jury?
Name one right of everyone living in the United States.
o: The right to hold federal office

questions.q: What do we show loyalty to when we say the Pledge of Allegiance?
o: The United States and the flag

questions.q: Name one promise new citizens make in the Oath of Allegiance.
o: Defend the Constitution and obey U.S. laws
Give up the right to vote

questions.q: How can people become U.S. citizens?
o: Be born in the U.S. or naturalize

questions.q: What is one example of civic participation?
What is one way Americans can serve their country?
o: Vote, pay taxes, or serve in the military

questions.q: Why is it important to pay federal taxes?
o: Required by law (16th Amendment) and funds the government

questions.q: Why must men age 18–25 register for the Selective Service?
o: Required by law; it makes a draft fair if ever needed

questions.q: Name one reason colonists came to America.
Who lived in America before the Europeans arrived?
What group of people was taken and sold as slaves?
What war did Americans fight to win independence from Britain?
Name one reason the Americans declared independence.
Who wrote the Declaration of Independence?
When was the Declaration of Independence adopted?
Name one important event of the American Revolution.
Which of these was one of the 13 original states?
What founding document was written in 1787?
Name one writer of the Federalist Papers.
Why were the Federalist Papers important?
Benjamin Franklin is famous for being…
o: A U.S. diplomat and the first Postmaster General
The author of the Constitution alone

questions.q: George Washington is famous for being…
o: The first President and 'Father of Our Country'

questions.q: Thomas Jefferson is famous for…
James Madison is famous as…
Alexander Hamilton is famous as…
o: The first Secretary of the Treasury

questions.q: What territory did the U.S. buy from France in 1803?
Name one war fought by the U.S. in the 1800s.
What was the U.S. war between the North and the South?
Name one important event of the Civil War.
Abraham Lincoln is famous for…
o: Freeing the slaves and preserving the Union

questions.q: What did the Emancipation Proclamation do?
o: Freed the slaves in the Confederate states

questions.q: What U.S. war ended slavery?
What amendment says all persons born or naturalized in the U.S. are citizens?
When did all men get the right to vote?
Name one leader of the women's rights movement in the 1800s.
Name one war fought by the U.S. in the 1900s.
Why did the U.S. enter World War I?
o: To support the Allies after Germany attacked U.S. ships
To stop the spread of communism

questions.q: When did all women get the right to vote?
What was the Great Depression?
o: The longest economic recession in modern history

questions.q: When did the Great Depression start?
Who was President during the Great Depression and World War II?
Why did the U.S. enter World War II?
o: Because of the bombing of Pearl Harbor

questions.q: Dwight Eisenhower is famous as…
o: A WWII general and the 34th President

questions.q: Who was the U.S.'s main rival during the Cold War?
During the Cold War, what was a main U.S. concern?
Why did the U.S. enter the Korean War?
o: To stop the spread of communism

questions.q: Why did the U.S. enter the Vietnam War?
o: To stop the spread of communism

questions.q: What did the civil rights movement do?
Martin Luther King, Jr. is famous for…
o: Fighting for civil rights and equality

questions.q: Why did the U.S. enter the Persian Gulf War?
o: To force the Iraqi military out of Kuwait

questions.q: What major event happened on September 11, 2001?
Name one U.S. military conflict after September 11, 2001.
o: The War in Afghanistan (War on Terror)

questions.q: Name one American Indian tribe in the United States.
Name one example of an American innovation.
What is the capital of the United States?
Where is the Statue of Liberty?
Why does the flag have 13 stripes?
Why does the flag have 50 stars?
What is the name of the national anthem?
What does the motto 'E Pluribus Unum' mean?
What is Independence Day?
Which of these is a national U.S. holiday?
What is Memorial Day?
o: A holiday to honor soldiers who died in military service
A holiday to honor all workers
A day to honor the flag

questions.q: What is Veterans Day?
o: A holiday to honor all who have served in the U.S. military
A holiday only for soldiers who died in service
